idea-plugin.name: SSH Tunnel Exporter
idea-plugin.description:
Generates the standalone ssh -L port-forwarding command for any data source that is configured with an SSH tunnel — so you can open the exact same bridge outside the IDE (for psql, mysql, a dump tool, or anything else).

  • Right-click a data source in the Database tool window → Copy SSH Tunnel Command, or use Tools → Copy SSH Tunnel Command… to pick one.
  • Reads the data source's SSH configuration (host, port, user, key) and the database host/port, then builds the command for you.
  • Editable local port and SSH option toggles — -N, -f, -C, -v, ConnectTimeout, IdentitiesOnly, ExitOnForwardFailure and keep-alive (ServerAliveInterval/ServerAliveCountMax) — reflected live in the command; one click to copy.
  • Or copy the equivalent ~/.ssh/config Host stanza, with an editable host alias (defaults to the SSH configuration name, else the data source name).
  • Shows a small “how it works” flow picture and, for known DBMS, the matching client command to connect through the tunnel (e.g. psql -h localhost -p 5432).
  • Never emits passwords — key-pair auth adds -i <key>; password/agent auth is left for ssh to resolve.

Works in DataGrip (and other JetBrains IDEs with the Database Tools & SQL plugin).
idea-plugin.change-notes:
• 0.3.0 — Add SSH option toggles (-N, -f, -C, -v, ConnectTimeout, IdentitiesOnly, ExitOnForwardFailure, and ServerAliveInterval/ServerAliveCountMax keep-alive), one-click copy of the equivalent ~/.ssh/config stanza with an editable host alias, a flow diagram, and a copyable native-client connect command for known DBMS.
  • 0.1.0 — Initial release: generate and copy the ssh -L tunnel command for a data source, from the Database tool window or the Tools menu, with a foreground/background toggle and an editable local port.

action.description: Generate the ssh -L command for this data source's SSH tunnel
Pick a data source and generate its ssh -L command
